stories.title: Trump's criticism of NATO raises questions of whether — or how — the U.S. could leave
stories.description: President Trump has complained that some NATO states aren't letting him use bases in the war against Iran. His criticism has left many wondering if the U.S. may try to withdraw from the alliance.

stories.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to get internet
stories.description: As the Israel and the U.S. continue striking Iran, Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey to find an Internet connection with access cut off in Iran

stories.title: A U.S. jet goes down over Iran, a U.S. official confirms
stories.description: A U.S. official said that one crew member had been rescued and U.S. forces continue to search for the second crew member.

stories.title: 2 U.S. planes are down and Iran hits Gulf refineries as the war wraps its 5th week
stories.description: An F-15 went down in Iran and a second Air Force plane crashed near the Strait of Hormuz as the war capped a week of intensified fighting.

stories.title: Evacuation of U.S. troops from Mideast base sends community groups scrambling to help
stories.description: Troops and their families have been pushed back to the United States after their bases in the Middle East were threatened by Iranian counterattacks. Community groups are scrambling to react.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz closure deflates global helium supply
stories.description: The blockade in the Strait of Hormuz is affecting not only oil, but the global helium supply.

stories.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

stories.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
stories.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

stories.title: When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
stories.description: Experts say many US presidents have offered a timeline for a conflict - only to then shift their estimates.

stories.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
stories.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

stories.title: 'This has got me worried': Iranians fear what comes next after US strike on Karaj bridge
stories.description: President Trump has warned Iran of strikes on bridges and electric power plants if its leaders do not agree to his terms to end the war.

stories.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
stories.description: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

stories.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
stories.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

stories.title: Iran's two largest steel plants shut down due to air strikes, companies say
stories.description: The strikes, which Iran says were first launched by Israel in co-ordination with the US last week, could cause major damage to the Iranian economy.

stories.title: 'Be serious... don't speak every day': Macron criticises Trump approach to Iran war
stories.description: The comments were an apparent jab at the US leader's sometimes contradictory stance on the US-Israeli war against Iran.

stories.title: Iranian Nobel laureate suffered suspected heart attack in prison, family says
stories.description: Narges Mohammadi's brother says he fears her life is in imminent danger and she needs to be transferred to a hospital.

stories.title: UK and allies discuss sanctions to stop Iran blocking Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Yvette Cooper said coordinated diplomatic and economic measures are required to reopen the shipping lane.

stories.title: Trump says Iran war objectives 'nearing completion' in address to nation
stories.description: The US president's first televised address of the conflict with Iran did little to quell uneasy markets.

stories.title: Israel intensifies Lebanon attacks and hits areas not in Hezbollah's control
stories.description: Attacks have continued as Israel has announced its intention to control swathes of south Lebanon.

stories.title: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
stories.description: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

stories.title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say
stories.description: An 11-year-old is reported to have been killed in an air strike while manning a checkpoint in Tehran.

stories.title: Two US fighter jets downed in Mideast, one crew member rescued
stories.description: A US fighter jet was shot down over Iranian airspace on Friday, triggering a search operation by Washington which rescued one crew member. A second US Air Force aircraft went down later in the day, according to a US official. The whereabouts of any other crew were not immediately...

stories.title: Trump seeks massive $1.5 trillion defence budget amid Iran war
stories.description: Engaged in a costly war against Iran, the White House unveiled its proposed budget for 2027 on Friday, asking Congress to increase the United States’ already massive defence spending by more than 40 percent over the course of a year. This would be a record in the country's recent...

stories.title: US fighter jet downed. Spencer Boyer speaks to France 24
stories.description: France 24 speaks to Spencer Boyer, former US Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense, about Iran’s claim that it shot down a US fighter jet.

stories.title: Iran: US fighter jet downed, War “It’s not a show”, The Space Race, Will Syrians leave Germany?
stories.description: It’s been a week that began with President Trump addressing the nation for the first time since the Iran War began five weeks ago. Expectations that he might signal an end to the conflict—or announce a ground offensive or a split from NATO—were unmet. Instead, his 18-minute speec...

stories.title: Baltic Sea strikes: How Ukraine aims to undermine Russia's oil profits from Iran war
stories.description: Ukraine has in recent days intensified drone attacks on ports and refineries in Russia’s Leningrad region on the Baltic Sea. Yet Kyiv’s strategy of trying to stop Moscow from reaping the benefits of oil disruption in the Middle East doesn’t come without significant risk.

stories.title: French-owned CMA CGM container ship passes Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: A container ship belonging to French shipping group CMA CGM has passed through the Strait of Hormuz, ​MarineTraffic vessel ‌tracking data shows, in a sign that Iran may not consider France ⁠a hostile nation. It is the first French-owned vessel to ‌make it through the channel sinc...

stories.title: On the ground: 'Massive explosion' rocks northern Tehran
stories.description: A large explosion shook northern Tehran on Friday, FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from the Iranian capital, with smoke seen rising from the site of the blast, as the Israeli military said it had launched a "wide-scale wave" of strikes, alongside parallel attacks in Beirut.

stories.title: Asian nations ramp up coal power amid Middle East energy crunch
stories.description: With Iran effectively turning the Strait of Hormuz into a toll gate, energy shortages are worsening across Asia. Amid the supply crunch, countries are increasingly turning back to coal for electricity, potentially slowing efforts to tackle climate change.

stories.title: Strikes hit Lebanon as Israel vows to occupy swathe of south after war
stories.description: Lebanon said Israeli strikes hit near Beirut's main airport road on Tuesday, as the UN Security Council held an urgent meeting after three peacekeepers were killed in the south. Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said his country's military would occupy a swathe of southern Leb...

stories.title: 8 killed, 95 wounded in US-Israeli attacks on Iran bridge
stories.description: At least eight civilians were killed and 95 others wounded in US and Israeli attacks on a bridge in Iran's northern Alborz province on Thursday, the official news agency IRNA reported. According to the report, the B1 bridge, located in the provincial capital Karaj, is one of the ...

stories.title: Iran`s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says it struck US F-35 jet
stories.description: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claimed Thursday that it struck a US F-35 stealth fighter jet over central Iran. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran, Iran.

stories.title: Iran Guards recruit children to ‘defend the homeland’, placing them in line of fire
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Corps has launched a campaign aimed at recruiting children as young as 12 for the “defence of the homeland”. More than a month into the war triggered by the US and Israel, rights groups highlight the life-threatening risks for Iranian children and stre...

stories.title: Iranian missile attack hits residential areas in central Israel
stories.description: An Iranian missile attack has caused damage across residential areas in central Israel, according to Israeli media.

stories.title: Iran war live: Tehran downs 2 US warplanes; Israel bombs Lebanon bridges
stories.description: Celebrations in Tehran as Iran's military says the strikes show its forces still have fighting capability.

stories.title: Will force be used to reopen Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Some countries threaten action against Iran's blockade of waterway.

stories.title: Survivors of Iran bridge attack recall terror of US-Israeli strike
stories.description: Survivors of an Israeli-US attack on Iran’s biggest road bridge have spoken of their terror as missiles hit it.

stories.title: More than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1: WHO
stories.description: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus says 'multiple attacks' on Iranian healthcare facilities have been reported.

stories.title: US unemployment rate drops despite economic uncertainty and Iran war
stories.description: White House says March jobs report 'blew out expectations' with gains made in the healthcare and construction sectors.

stories.title: What we know about the US fighter jet shot down in Iran
stories.description: The White House has confirmed Donald Trump has been briefed on the shooting down of a US fighter jet in Iran.

stories.title: Omani, French and Japanese vessels transit the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: CMA CGM's vessel becomes the first French-owned ship to traverse Hormuz since the US–Israel war on Iran began.

stories.title: Drone footage shows a factory in Israel damaged by Iranian missile debris
stories.description: Drone footage from Israel shows damage to a factory in Petah Tikva from debris from an intercepted Iranian missile.

stories.title: UNSC divided as Gulf pushes to reopen Hormuz
stories.description: • Vote on Bahrain-led, US-backed resolution postponed twice• Russia, China raise objections to certain parts• Iran warns against ‘provocative move’ UNITED NATIONS: The UN Security Council remains sharply divided over a Gulf Arab initiative to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the stra...

stories.title: 10 killed as Russia launches daytime attacks on Ukraine
stories.description: KYIV: Russian strikes killed at least 10 people in Ukraine on Friday, officials said, as Moscow launched the latest in an increasing number of daytime barrages. Moscow has been firing aerial broadsides at Ukraine throughout its more than four-year invasion, mostly at night, but i...

stories.title: ‘Silence’ stonewalls Pakistan-led US-Iran talks initiative
stories.description: ISLAMABAD: A Pakistan-led initiative to facilitate US-Iran talks has been held up, with Tehran yet to formally respond to proposals for dialogue despite back channel exchanges in recent days, a senior official told Dawn on Friday. The official, who was speaking on background, sai...

stories.title: Four ships cross Hormuz as Iran says it is drafting protocol
stories.description: LONDON: Four ships, including one co-owned by a Japanese company and one by a French company, crossed the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday as Iran said it is drafting a peacetime protocol to supervise maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz with Oman. The near closing of the St...

stories.title: Putin, Erdogan call for immediate ceasefire in Middle East war
stories.description: • Leaders warn of ‘serious’ global consequences for energy, trade, logistics• Talks also cover coordinated security measures for the Black Sea area MOSCOW: Russian Pre­si­dent Vladimir Putin and Turkish counterpart Recep Tayyip Erdogan called for an immediate ceasefire in the Mid...

stories.title: White House seeks $1.5tr defence budget as war on Iran drives costs
stories.description: United States President Donald Trump asked lawmakers on Friday to approve a massive $1.5 trillion defence budget for 2027, as the country faces rising costs from its war on Iran and mounting global security commitments. The proposal would lift Pentagon spending by more than 40 pe...

stories.title: Iran’s military claims shooting down second US jet while hunt for crew of downed F-15 aircraft continues
stories.description: Iranian and American forces were racing each other early Saturday to recover the crew of the first US fighter jet to go down inside Iran since the start of the war. Tehran said it had shot down the F-15 warplane, while US media reported American special forces had rescued one of ...

stories.title: Pentagon shake-up as Hegseth ousts army chief amid Iran conflict
stories.description: WASHINGTON: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth has abruptly forced Army Chief of Staff General Randy George into immediate retirement amid the ongoing war with Iran, multiple US media outlets reported on Friday. The outlets described the move as one of the most consequential Penta...

stories.title: US blows up key Iranian bridge, steel plants
stories.description: • Trump says more will follow after earlier threatening to bomb Iran back to ‘Stone Ages’, claims war nearly over• Tehran vows ‘crushing’ attacks on US after Trump threats• Iran launches missiles towards Israel; fire near Mashhad airport after projectile hits fuel tank• Dozens of...

stories.title: Gulf states seek UN mandate for force to protect Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The secretary general of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) on Thursday called for the UN Security Council to authorise the use of force to protect the Strait of Hormuz from Iranian attacks. Iran has placed a stranglehold on the key shipping lane — threatening fuel supplies and b...

stories.title: Macron opposes operation to ‘liberate’ Strait, calls it ‘unrealistic’
stories.description: SEOUL: French President Emmanuel Macron said on Thursday that a military operation to liberate the Strait of Hormuz is “unrealistic”, while lamenting Donald Trump’s differing daily statements on the Iran war and Nato. “There are those who advocate for the liberation of the Strait...

stories.title: Luxury stores take hit in Dubai as Iran war drags on
stories.description: DUBAI: Rows of luxury boutiques line an upmarket Dubai mall, yet a month into the Middle East war their sales staff sit idly, phones in hand, watching for the rare customer. Salespeople in immaculate suits say they have been told not to speak to reporters, but one briefly describ...

stories.title: Lebanese PM decries Israel’s ‘far-reaching’ occupation goals
stories.description: • Salam calls Lebanon ‘a victim of war’; condemns Tel Aviv’s ‘dangerous talk’ of buffer zones• 18 European nations demand Israel halt its military campaign, respect Lebanon’s sovereignty BEIRUT: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam declared on Thursday there was no end in sight to...

stories.title: Middle East conflict spiralling into wider regional war, warns UN chief
stories.description: WASHINGTON: UN Secretary General Antó­nio Guterres warned on Thursday that the Middle East crisis was spiralling into a wider regional war, urging the United States and Israel to halt hostilities and calling on Iran to stop attacks on its neighbours. Addressing reporters at UN He...

stories.title: UN experts call for investigation into killing of 3 Lebanese journalists in Israeli strike
stories.description: UN experts have called for an international investigation into the killing of three Lebanese journalists in an Israeli strike, saying Israel had not provided “credible evidence” of their supposed links to armed groups. The three journalists, including Al Manar correspondent Ali S...

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
